_comment: Benchmark sweep configuration for the Quicksilver Monte Carlo particle transport proxy app.
Each scenario maps to one BenchmarkScenario object in metrics_collector.py.
Application: Quicksilver (ECP proxy for Mercury)
Executable:  qs
Input file:  Examples/ValidationMixed/validationMixed.inp
- nGroups=1  (single energy group – avoids OOM from large cross-section tables)
- nParticles=100000
- nSteps=20  (runs in ~12s with 2 MPI ranks on a 4-core machine)
- 90% scattering / 10% absorption (creates long particle tracks for realistic workload)
- 10x10x10 mesh with xDom=0 yDom=0 zDom=0 (auto domain decomposition)
IMPORTANT: Quicksilver reads the input file AFTER CLI args, so CLI overrides for
nSteps/nParticles are silently overwritten by the input file values.
All workload parameters must be set in the input file itself.
NOTE: Do NOT pass --xDom/--yDom/--zDom overrides.
Explicit domain decomposition overrides cause segfaults with this input's mesh dimensions.
Let Quicksilver auto-decompose (xDom=0 yDom=0 zDom=0 from the input file).
Design:
- Fixed 2 MPI processes for all scenarios (leaves 2 CPUs free on a 4-core machine).
- Single workload size (nSteps=20, ~12s runtime) controlled by the input file.
- 1 failure-free baseline + 2 failure scenarios with injection delays of 4s and 8s.
- Total: 3 scenarios, 1 run each (fast smoke-test friendly).
- Injection delays are chosen to be within the ~12s run window.
- 4s injection: kills at ~33% through (early failure, many cycles to recover)
- 8s injection: kills at ~67% through (late failure, few cycles to recover)
Set INPUT_FILE env var to override the default input file path.
Default: build/ecp/Quicksilver/Examples/ValidationMixed/validationMixed.inp
(resolved via INPUT_FILE env var in run_quicksilver_validation.sh)

scenarios._comment: === Failure injection at 4s (early in run, ~33% through) ===
scenarios.name: failure-delay-4s

scenarios._comment: === Failure injection at 8s (late in run, ~67% through) ===
scenarios.name: failure-delay-8s
